clusters.title: Sri Lanka to see slow growth, widening fiscal deficit: Fitch

versions.title: Sri Lanka to see slow growth, widening fiscal deficit: Fitch
versions.deck: Fitch Ratings has said that Sri Lanka’s recovery remains fragile despite improved macroeconomic fundamentals, with the Middle East energy shock now testing the gains achieved through stabilisation and reforms. In its latest Sri Lanka update on credit development, Fitch said growth is expected to slow, inflation to rise and the fiscal deficit to widen in 2026, even as the economy builds on stronger fundamentals following the 2022 crisis. clusters.title: PDMO gains global recognition with prestigious Commonwealth Award

versions.title: PDMO gains global recognition with prestigious Commonwealth Award
versions.deck: Sri Lanka has achieved a landmark milestone in its public debt management reform journey, with the Public Debt Management Office (PDMO) being awarded “Debt Management Office of the Year (Asia-Pacific)” at the Commonwealth Debt Management Award Ceremony held at Marlborough House, London, on 26 March, in parallel with the Commonwealth Debt Management Forum held from 23–27 March.

clusters.title: Sri Lanka pushes to centre unpaid care work in economic policy

versions.title: Sri Lanka pushes to centre unpaid care work in economic policy
versions.deck: By Charumini de Silva At a time when South Asia is rethinking labour markets and social protection, Prime Minister Dr. Harini Amarasuriya yesterday called for a “fundamental rethinking” of how economies define productivity, urging policymakers to recognise unpaid care work as a core pillar of economic systems.

clusters.title: Abans Finance gets CSE nod for Rs. 1.5 b debt issue

versions.title: Abans Finance gets CSE nod for Rs. 1.5 b debt issue
versions.deck: The Colombo Stock Exchange yesterday said it has granted in-principle approval for the listing of proposed debt securities of Abans Finance PLC. The proposed issue comprises an initial tranche of 10 million listed, rated, senior, unsecured, redeemable five-year (2026/2031) debentures at a face value of Rs. 100 each. The company has the option to issue a further 5 million debentures in the event of an oversubscription, taking the total issue size

versions.deck: After a sustained 21-day selling streak, foreign investors in the Colombo stock market yesterday reversed course to emerge as net buyers. The net foreign inflow amounted to Rs. 19.8 million.

clusters.title: World Bank sees Sri Lanka growth slowing to 3.6% in 2026 amid energy shock

versions.title: World Bank sees Sri Lanka growth slowing to 3.6% in 2026 amid energy shock
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s economic growth is expected to slow to 3.6% in 2026 as higher energy prices weigh on activity, although the economy will regain its pre-crisis size last seen in 2018, the World Bank said in its latest South Asia Economic Update April 2026.

clusters.title: Philip Gunawardena and his positive mindset: Speeches in the last lap of Parliament

versions.title: Philip Gunawardena and his positive mindset: Speeches in the last lap of Parliament
versions.deck: The Philip Gunawardena Commemorative Society has released Volume III of the speeches of Philip Gunawardena in Parliament from 1965 to 1970. Previously, the Commemorative Society had issued a compilation of his speeches in the State Council during the colonial period as Volume I, and those in Parliament in independent Sri Lanka as Volume II.

clusters.title: Seven deadly sins of Sri Lanka’s dastardly 2019 Easter bombings

versions.title: Seven deadly sins of Sri Lanka’s dastardly 2019 Easter bombings
versions.deck: Seven years on from that black Easter Sunday of 2019, the toll of grief has not let up its death grip. Nor have the traumatic memories of the dark day which unshrouded terror diminished. The wound has merely deepened into something more corrosive and even cancerous – since it has been left untreated.

clusters.title: End of corporate shadows: Mandatory Beneficial Ownership disclosure begins

versions.title: End of corporate shadows: Mandatory Beneficial Ownership disclosure begins
versions.deck: Sri Lanka is witnessing a landmark change in its corporate governance as the new beneficial ownership rules take effect. For decades, the true individuals pulling the strings of complex corporate structures could remain hidden behind layers of holding companies and nominee shareholders.
